findings.title: Async operation without Suspense boundary
findings.description: Async Server Components without Suspense block the entire page from rendering until data loads.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap async components in <Suspense fallback={<Loading />}>. Create loading.tsx for automatic loading UI.

findings.title: Async operation without Suspense boundary
findings.description: Async Server Components without Suspense block the entire page from rendering until data loads.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap async components in <Suspense fallback={<Loading />}>. Create loading.tsx for automatic loading UI.

findings.title: Array index used as key prop
findings.description: Using array index as key causes incorrect component reuse when items are added, removed, or reordered. This causes UI bugs and lost state.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a stable unique identifier: key={item.id} or key={item.slug}

findings.title: Non-null assertion operator (!)
findings.description: The ! operator tells TypeScript to ignore null/undefined. If the value is actually null, you get a runtime crash.
findings.suggested_fix: Use optional chaining (?.) with a fallback: value?.property ?? defaultValue

findings.title: Async operation without Suspense boundary
findings.description: Async Server Components without Suspense block the entire page from rendering until data loads.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap async components in <Suspense fallback={<Loading />}>. Create loading.tsx for automatic loading UI.

findings.description: Async Server Components without Suspense block the entire page from rendering until data loads.

findings.title: Async operation without Suspense boundary
findings.description: Async Server Components without Suspense block the entire page from rendering until data loads.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap async components in <Suspense fallback={<Loading />}>. Create loading.tsx for automatic loading UI.

findings.title: Array index used as key prop
findings.description: Using array index as key causes incorrect component reuse when items are added, removed, or reordered. This causes UI bugs and lost state.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a stable unique identifier: key={item.id} or key={item.slug}

findings.title: Direct state mutation
findings.description: Directly mutating state object 'reviews' doesn't trigger a re-render and causes subtle bugs.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the setter function with a new object: setReviews(prev => ({...prev, field: value}))

findings.title: useEffect missing dependency array
findings.description: useEffect without a dependency array runs after every render. This causes infinite loops if the effect updates state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array: useEffect(() => { ... }, [dep1, dep2]). Use [] for mount-only effects.

findings.title: Async operation without error handling
findings.description: Async operations without try/catch or .catch() will throw unhandled promise rejections that crash the component.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap in try/catch or use React Query/SWR which handles errors automatically. Add an error boundary for component-level errors.

findings.title: Spreading props without explicit typing
findings.description: Spreading ...props passes all props including internal ones to DOM elements, causing React warnings and unexpected behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Destructure only needed props: const { onClick, className, ...rest } = props

findings.title: Spreading props without explicit typing
findings.description: Spreading ...props passes all props including internal ones to DOM elements, causing React warnings and unexpected behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Destructure only needed props: const { onClick, className, ...rest } = props

findings.title: Unsafe type assertion (as)
findings.description: Direct type assertion bypasses TypeScript's type checker. If the actual runtime type doesn't match, you get silent corruption.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a type guard function or parse with zod/valibot for runtime validation before asserting the type.

findings.title: Async operation without error handling
findings.description: Async operations without try/catch or .catch() will throw unhandled promise rejections that crash the component.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap in try/catch or use React Query/SWR which handles errors automatically. Add an error boundary for component-level errors.

findings.description: Spreading ...props passes all props including internal ones to DOM elements, causing React warnings and unexpected behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Destructure only needed props: const { onClick, className, ...rest } = props

findings.title: Component file too large
findings.description: This component has 373 non-empty lines. Large components are hard to test, understand, and maintain.
findings.suggested_fix: Split into smaller focused components. Extract hooks into custom hooks. Aim for components under 200 lines.

findings.title: Array index used as key prop
findings.description: Using array index as key causes incorrect component reuse when items are added, removed, or reordered. This causes UI bugs and lost state.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a stable unique identifier: key={item.id} or key={item.slug}

findings.title: useEffect missing dependency array
findings.description: useEffect without a dependency array runs after every render. This causes infinite loops if the effect updates state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array: useEffect(() => { ... }, [dep1, dep2]). Use [] for mount-only effects.

findings.title: useEffect missing dependency array
findings.description: useEffect without a dependency array runs after every render. This causes infinite loops if the effect updates state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array: useEffect(() => { ... }, [dep1, dep2]). Use [] for mount-only effects.

findings.title: Array index used as key prop
findings.description: Using array index as key causes incorrect component reuse when items are added, removed, or reordered. This causes UI bugs and lost state.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a stable unique identifier: key={item.id} or key={item.slug}

findings.title: Hardcoded URL instead of environment variable
findings.description: Hardcoded URLs break when deploying to different environments (dev/staging/prod) and expose internal infrastructure.
findings.suggested_fix: Use environment variables: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL. Add to .env.local and .env.example.

findings.title: Hardcoded URL instead of environment variable
findings.description: Hardcoded URLs break when deploying to different environments (dev/staging/prod) and expose internal infrastructure.
findings.suggested_fix: Use environment variables: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL. Add to .env.local and .env.example.

findings.title: Hardcoded URL instead of environment variable
findings.description: Hardcoded URLs break when deploying to different environments (dev/staging/prod) and expose internal infrastructure.
findings.suggested_fix: Use environment variables: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL. Add to .env.local and .env.example.

findings.title: useMemo/useCallback missing dependency array
findings.description: useMemo and useCallback require a dependency array. Without it, memoization is disabled and runs every render.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array as the second argument: useMemo(() => compute(a, b), [a, b])

findings.title: useMemo/useCallback missing dependency array
findings.description: useMemo and useCallback require a dependency array. Without it, memoization is disabled and runs every render.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array as the second argument: useMemo(() => compute(a, b), [a, b])

findings.title: useMemo/useCallback missing dependency array

findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array as the second argument: useMemo(() => compute(a, b), [a, b])

findings.title: useMemo/useCallback missing dependency array
findings.description: useMemo and useCallback require a dependency array. Without it, memoization is disabled and runs every render.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array as the second argument: useMemo(() => compute(a, b), [a, b])

findings.title: useEffect missing dependency array
findings.description: useEffect without a dependency array runs after every render. This causes infinite loops if the effect updates state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array: useEffect(() => { ... }, [dep1, dep2]). Use [] for mount-only effects.

findings.title: useEffect missing dependency array
findings.description: useEffect without a dependency array runs after every render. This causes infinite loops if the effect updates state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array: useEffect(() => { ... }, [dep1, dep2]). Use [] for mount-only effects.

findings.title: Data fetching in client component with useEffect
findings.description: In Next.js App Router, data fetching in useEffect runs only on the client, missing SSR benefits, causing loading flashes, and duplicating requests.
findings.suggested_fix: Move data fetching to a Server Component (remove 'use client') or use React Query/SWR for client-side fetching with caching.

findings.title: Data fetching in client component with useEffect
findings.description: In Next.js App Router, data fetching in useEffect runs only on the client, missing SSR benefits, causing loading flashes, and duplicating requests.
findings.suggested_fix: Move data fetching to a Server Component (remove 'use client') or use React Query/SWR for client-side fetching with caching.

findings.title: Array index used as key prop
findings.description: Using array index as key causes incorrect component reuse when items are added, removed, or reordered. This causes UI bugs and lost state.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a stable unique identifier: key={item.id} or key={item.slug}

findings.title: console.log/warn/error left in code
findings.description: Console statements left in production code leak implementation details and clutter browser devtools.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove debug logs. Use a proper logging library or environment-gated logging.

findings.title: Server Action without input validation
findings.description: Server Actions receive data directly from the client. Without validation, malicious users can send unexpected data.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate input with Zod before processing: const data = schema.parse(formData)

findings.title: Server Action without cache revalidation
findings.description: Mutating data without revalidating the cache means the UI shows stale data after the action completes.
findings.suggested_fix: Call revalidatePath('/path') or revalidateTag('tag') after mutations to refresh the cache.

findings.title: Server Action without cache revalidation

findings.title: Server Action without input validation
findings.description: Server Actions receive data directly from the client. Without validation, malicious users can send unexpected data.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate input with Zod before processing: const data = schema.parse(formData)

findings.title: Server Action without cache revalidation
findings.description: Mutating data without revalidating the cache means the UI shows stale data after the action completes.
findings.suggested_fix: Call revalidatePath('/path') or revalidateTag('tag') after mutations to refresh the cache.

findings.title: Explicit 'any' type usage
findings.description: Using 'any' defeats TypeScript's type safety. It spreads unsafety to all callers and disables autocomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: Use 'unknown' for truly unknown types (requires type narrowing), or define a specific interface/type.

findings.title: Async operation without error handling
findings.description: Async operations without try/catch or .catch() will throw unhandled promise rejections that crash the component.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap in try/catch or use React Query/SWR which handles errors automatically. Add an error boundary for component-level errors.

findings.title: SQL Injection via Prisma findFirst
findings.description: The email parameter from credentials is directly passed to Prisma's findFirst query without proper validation or sanitization. While Prisma provides some protection, the lack of input validation could still lead to unexpected behavior or potential injection if the email field contains malicious input.
findings.suggested_fix: Add proper email validation before the database query: validate email format using a regex or email validation library, and implement input sanitization.

findings.title: Overly Long Session Duration
findings.description: The session maxAge is set to 30 days (30 * 24 * 60 * 60), which is excessively long for a JWT-based authentication system. This increases the window of opportunity for session hijacking and reduces security if tokens are compromised.
findings.suggested_fix: Reduce session duration to a more secure timeframe (e.g., 1-7 days) and implement refresh token mechanism for better user experience while maintaining security.

findings.title: Missing Rate Limiting for Authentication
findings.description: The authentication endpoint lacks rate limiting, making it vulnerable to brute force attacks. Attackers could repeatedly attempt to guess passwords without being throttled.
findings.suggested_fix: Implement rate limiting using middleware or a library like express-rate-limit to limit authentication attempts per IP address (e.g., 5 attempts per 15 minutes).

findings.title: Information Disclosure through Timing Attack
findings.description: The code structure allows for timing attacks where an attacker can determine if an email exists in the database based on response times. The password comparison only occurs if the user exists, creating timing differences.
findings.suggested_fix: Always perform password comparison even for non-existent users using a dummy hash to maintain consistent response times, or implement account enumeration protection.

findings.title: Missing Error Handling and Logging
findings.description: There's no proper error handling for database operations or authentication failures. This could lead to unhandled exceptions and lacks security logging for monitoring suspicious activities.
findings.suggested_fix: Add try-catch blocks around database operations, implement proper error logging for failed authentication attempts, and ensure sensitive information is not exposed in error messages.

findings.title: Missing Security Headers Configuration
findings.description: The NextAuth configuration doesn't explicitly set security-related options like secure cookies, SameSite attributes, or CSRF protection, which could be important for production environments.
findings.suggested_fix: Add explicit cookie configuration with secure: true, sameSite: 'strict', and ensure proper CSRF protection is enabled in production.
